changes: OpenClaw shipped v2026.4.9 on 2026-04-09 at 02:25 UTC, after a short-lived v2026.4.9-beta.1 at 01:26 UTC.
PicoClaw published v0.2.6 on 2026-04-08 at 13:39 UTC, keeping the cheap-hardware and edge lane active.
Hermes Agent published v2026.4.8 / v0.8.0 on 2026-04-08 at 11:56 UTC, extending the learning-loop adjacent benchmark's release momentum.
The broader atlas structure is unchanged today: the monitored set, taxonomy, and executive read still hold.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw remains the highest-signal mover in the category, and another same-day release keeps the reference product in pace-setting territory.
PicoClaw continuing to ship reinforces that the lightweight edge-hardware lane is not just a one-week spike.
Hermes shipping again matters because the strongest learning-loop adjacent keeps compounding, not stalling.
Net: this is meaningful operating movement, but still not enough to rewrite the map or executive thesis.
